_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates districts 88-92 (state tier). Don Scott (Speaker, D-88), Kacey Carnegie (D-89), Jay Leftwich (R-90), Cliff Hayes Jr. (D-91), Bonita Anthony (D-92). Evidence-based finalization from primary roll calls (LIS / VPAP / RichmondSunlight), ReproRising 2025 scorecard, Giffords endorsements, and campaign-finance records. Leftwich: uniformly conservative HARD record (NO on both the same-sex-marriage and reproductive-freedom amendments at committee + floor, both references; NOT a GOP crossover). Scott/Carnegie/Hayes: progressive HARD records. Anthony: thinner — individual roll calls were not fetchable (LIS/VPAP blocked), so only the named-to-her HARD signals (ReproRising A+ Champion, Giffords champion endorsement) are scored; marriage/other cells left null rather than inferred from party.

set.office: Speaker of the Virginia House of Delegates (District 88)

profile.religion: Episcopalian (per Ballotpedia profile aggregation; not a first-person statement)
profile.background: Speaker of the Virginia House of Delegates and Delegate for District 88 (Portsmouth); in the House since 2020, elected Speaker Jan 2024 (first Black Speaker in Virginia history), re-elected Speaker after the 2025 Democratic majority. Portsmouth trial attorney and Navy veteran (LSU Law 1994).

don-scott.notes_set: VA House Speaker, HD-88 (Portsmouth); attorney, Navy veteran. [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Progressive leadership record. As Speaker he presides and customarily does not cast floor votes, so the HARD evidence here is his public advocacy + the measures his majority advanced: publicly championed HJ1 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment, House 64-34, 01/2025) and named 'marriage equality' a top priority while HJ9 (same-sex-marriage amendment) passed 58-35 under his leadership; backed Gov. Spanberger's order ending state-police ICE cooperation; Giffords-endorsed and credited with championing the assault-weapons ban, universal background checks, one-handgun-a-month, and red-flag laws. School-choice/parental-rights, conscience, election-mechanics, and 10th-Amendment cells left NULL — no Scott-specific roll call or statement found (Speaker-abstention convention makes individual votes sparse).

0.note: Publicly championed HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34, 01/14/2025); leads the Democratic majority that advanced it. Does not affirm personhood from conception.
Named 'marriage equality' a top priority he was safeguarding; HJ9 (same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment) passed the House 58-35 (01/14/2025) under his leadership.

1.note: Champions codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ9).

2.note: Backed Gov. Spanberger's executive order ending Virginia State Police cooperation with ICE — opposite of opposing sanctuary-type policies.

1.note: Giffords-endorsed; credited on its candidate page with championing the assault-weapons ban, universal background checks, one-handgun-a-month, and 'extreme risk' (red-flag) laws.

profile.religion: Former Trustee, Deep Creek United Methodist Church (Chesapeake) — per Ballotpedia affiliations; not a first-person faith statement
profile.background: Freshman Delegate for District 89 (Chesapeake/Suffolk); flipped a Republican-held open seat Nov 2025, took office Jan 14, 2026. Chesapeake probate/estates attorney (Univ. of Richmond Law 2008) who founded her own firm in 2012; sits on Privileges & Elections, Courts of Justice, and Public Safety.

kacey-carnegie.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-89 (Chesapeake/Suffolk); attorney; freshman elected Nov 2025, seated Jan 2026 — already has a full 2026-session voting record. [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Progressive HARD record from her first session: voted YES on HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment) and HB6 (right to contraception); endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA and EMILY's List, with PP ($46,334) and EMILY's List ($22,075) among her donors; named co-patron of HJ3 (same-sex-marriage amendment, House 67-31); voted to expand absentee voting (HB82) and felon re-enfranchisement (HJ2); voted the gun-control slate (HB19/HB21/HB40/HB93); cast criminal-justice-leniency votes (HB26 marijuana resentencing, HB17 limiting court-fine collection). Family/child-sovereignty cells left NULL — she left the iVoterGuide questionnaire blank, so its auto-generated 'school choice' label is not a stated position. Conscience, federal-overreach, and border cells NULL (no record found).

0.note: Voted YES on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34, 01/14/2026); pledged to 'proudly support' it. Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted YES on HB6 establishing a right to obtain contraception, alongside the pro-abortion-access slate — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Accepted abortion-industry PAC funding: Planned Parenthood VA ($46,334) and EMILY's List ($22,075) among her top donors; endorsed by both.

0.note: Named co-patron of HJ3, the marriage-equality constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's man-woman marriage definition (House 67-31, 01/14/2026).

1.note: Co-patron of the amendment codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3).

3.note: Voted YES on HB82, extending the absentee-ballot receipt deadline — expands mail-in voting rather than restricting it.

1.note: Voted YES on HB26 (marijuana sentence resentencing/modification) and HB17 (limiting collection of court fines/costs) — criminal-justice-leniency votes.
Voted YES on the 2026 gun-control slate: HB19 (firearm prohibition after family assault), HB21 (firearm-industry civil liability), HB40 (ban on plastic firearms), HB93 (transfer restrictions).

profile.religion: Presbyterian; former elder, Great Bridge Presbyterian Church (Chesapeake) — per Ballotpedia/LegiStorm bio aggregation
profile.background: Republican Delegate for District 90 (Chesapeake); in the House continuously since Jan 2014 (HD-78 2014-2024, HD-90 since 2024); serves on Courts of Justice and Privileges & Elections. Attorney (Univ. of Richmond Law 1988); former Chesapeake School Board member and chair.

jay-leftwich.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-90 (Chesapeake); attorney; in the House since 2014; 91% GOP-caucus voting unity (VPAP). [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly conservative HARD record verified against LIS roll calls — NOT a GOP crossover on either amendment: voted NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9 2025 + HJ3 2026, committee AND floor) and NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1 2025 + 2026); ReproRising 2025 grade 'F'. Pro-parental-rights (HB2432 gender-incongruence notification), pro-school-choice (HB346 lab schools), protects minors from harmful online material (SB1515); kept photo voter ID (NO on HB1004 repeal) and opposed no-excuse absentee (NO on HB1); voted NO on all three 2020 gun-control bills (background checks HB2, red-flag HB674, assault-weapons ban HB961); tough-on-crime per his campaign platform. Economic, conscience, and 10th-Amendment cells left NULL — tax-cut votes don't document a balanced-budget position, and no conscience/nullification roll call surfaced. Border left NULL (only an in-state-tuition vote, which maps to no specific rubric cell). NRA 'A' grade was search-summary-only and excluded as unverified.

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1) at both the 2025 first reference (House 51-48) and the 2026 second reference (64-34), at committee and on the floor; ReproRising 2025 grade 'F'.
Voted NO on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment at both references (HJ9 2025, HJ3 2026), committee and floor; NOT among the GOP crossovers. Affirms marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted NO on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3, House 67-31).

2.note: Voted YES on HB2432 (2023), requiring parental notification when a minor experiences 'gender incongruence' — biological-sex framing on school policy.

0.note: Voted YES on HB346 (2022), expanding college-partnership laboratory schools — a school-choice expansion.

1.note: Voted YES on HB2432 — parental notification/consent on a minor's gender-related school interventions.

3.note: Voted YES on SB1515 (2023), civil liability for distributing material harmful to minors online (passed 96-0) — protects minors from sexualized content.

1.note: Voted NO on HB1004 (2020), which repealed the photo voter-ID requirement — i.e., voted to keep photo voter ID.

3.note: Voted NO on HB1 (2020) establishing no-excuse absentee voting — opposed expanded mail-in voting.

0.note: Publicly pro-law-enforcement on his campaign issues page; sponsored HB2194 criminalizing written threats. Affirmative tough-on-crime advocacy.

1.note: Opposes ending mandatory minimums and parole abolition — against soft-on-crime policy.
Voted NO on all three marquee 2020 gun-control bills: HB2 (universal background checks), HB674 (red-flag/ERPO), HB961 (assault-weapons ban).

profile.religion: Baptist; past member and trustee of Mount Zion Baptist Church (Chesapeake) — per his campaign bio
profile.background: Democratic Delegate for District 91 (Chesapeake); in the House since Jan 2017 (HD-77 2017-2024, HD-91 since 2024); former Chesapeake City Council member. IT executive (former CIO roles); chairs the Communications, Technology & Innovation Committee and an Appropriations subcommittee.

cliff-hayes-jr.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-91 (Chesapeake); IT executive; in the House since 2017; 99% Democratic-caucus voting unity (VPAP). [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Despite a faith-oriented reputation, his floor record is uniformly progressive with no documented caucus deviation. HARD: voted YES on HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment) and HB6 (contraception) with a ReproRising A+ 'Champion' grade; voted YES on HJ3 (same-sex-marriage amendment, confirmed by name in the roll call); voted to expand absentee/provisional-ballot cure (SB582) and felon re-enfranchisement (HJ2); cast consistent criminal-justice-leniency votes (HB246/HB489/HB172/HB247/HB933); voted YES on HB650 prohibiting ICE arrests at courthouses/schools (a sanctuary-type protection); Giffords-endorsed and credited with leading the 2020 secure-storage law and a 72-hour gun-purchase waiting period. The 'moderate/faith' framing rests on persona and committee portfolio, not dissenting votes. School-choice/curriculum, conscience, economic-rubric, and 10th-Amendment cells left NULL (no clean-fit roll call; an HJ440 book-removal STUDY vote was too procedural to score).

0.note: Voted YES on HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment, House 64-34, 2026); ReproRising 2025 grade A+ 'Champion'. Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted YES on HB6 (right to contraception) and the pro-abortion-access slate — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted YES on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (House 67-31, 2026) — confirmed by name in the roll-call 'Yes' list.

1.note: Voted YES to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3).

3.note: Voted YES on SB582 (expanding absentee/provisional-ballot cure) and HJ2 (felon-voting-rights restoration) — expands voting access rather than tightening mail-in/early voting.

1.note: Consistent criminal-justice-leniency votes: HB246 (reduced penalty/affirmative defense for mental illness), HB489 (restorative justice), HB247 (deferred disposition), HB933.

2.note: Voted YES on HB650 (2026) prohibiting federal immigration (ICE) arrests at courthouses, polling places, schools and hospitals — a sanctuary-type protection.

1.note: Giffords-endorsed; credited with leading the 2020 secure-firearm-storage law and a 72-hour gun-purchase waiting period, plus the 2025-26 gun-control slate (assault-firearms ban, red-flag SROs, industry liability).

profile.religion: Ordained Christian minister; M.Div (2002), Samuel DeWitt Proctor School of Theology, Virginia Union University; minister at Faith Deliverance Christian Center (Norfolk)
profile.background: Democratic Delegate for District 92 (Norfolk); first elected Nov 2023 (seated Jan 2024), re-elected Nov 2025. Electrical/aviation engineer and university administrator — director of engineering student success at Old Dominion University.

bonita-anthony.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-92 (Norfolk); ordained minister and ODU engineer-administrator; in the House since 2024. [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] THIN evidence record — VPAP, LIS, Richmond Sunlight, LegiScan and Ballotpedia all returned 403/JS-blocked this pass, so her individual roll calls could not be confirmed. Only signals tied to her BY NAME are scored: ReproRising 2025 A+ 'Champion' grade ('Anthony, Bonita — A+') + Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA endorsement + her own public statement that the Dobbs leak inspired her to run 'to protect abortion access' (sanctity_of_life FALSE); and Giffords PAC naming her a 'gun safety champion (HD-92)' (self_defense FALSE). The same-sex-marriage amendment passed with the all-Democrat bloc she belongs to, but her individual roll-call line was NOT retrievable, so marriage and all other dimensions are left NULL rather than inferred from party. Recommend a browser-based pass to confirm her individual votes and upgrade the denominator.

0.note: ReproRising 2025 A+ 'Champion' grade (listed 'Anthony, Bonita — A+'); publicly states the Dobbs leak inspired her to run 'to protect abortion access.' Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: A+ Champion grade is tied to voting the full reproductive-freedom slate; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia — opposite of abortion abolition.
Endorsed by Giffords PAC as a 'gun safety champion,' listed explicitly as 'Bonita Anthony (HD-92).'
